prerequisites: An active SAP Fieldglass account with access to the Configuration Manager (the self-service integration administration tool).
Permission in Configuration Manager to view and create entries in the API Application Keys table.

authMethods.name: API application key (x-api-key)
authMethods.summary: SAP Fieldglass REST APIs authenticate with an API application key. Create the key in SAP Fieldglass Configuration Manager under API Application Keys, then supply its value as the connector's api_key. SAP passes this value to the API in the x-api-key request header. Note that SAP's full REST flow also exchanges the API key plus an integration (web service) user for a short-lived OAuth bearer token at /api/oauth2/v2.0/token; this connector only collects the API application key.

setupSteps.title: Open Configuration Manager
setupSteps.body: Sign in to SAP Fieldglass and open Configuration Manager, the self-service tool used to administer integrations. Navigate to the API Application Keys area.

setupSteps.title: Create an API application key
setupSteps.body: In the API Application Keys table, create a new API application key (for example via the Basic Setup / New form). Here you can create, view, edit, enable, and disable keys. Copy the generated API application key value.

setupSteps.title: Provide the key to the connector
setupSteps.body: Set the connector's required api_key config field to the API application key value you copied. This is the connector's only secret; it is sent to SAP Fieldglass as the x-api-key header when calling the REST APIs.

sources.title: API Application Keys - SAP Help Portal (SAP Fieldglass Integration)
